summary: Verify all existing tests pass on the base commit before any changes. Run `cargo test -p reify-eval -p reify-runtime`.; F16 — Write test: concurrent eval with MetaAccess expression. Add `concurrent_eval_meta_access_resolves` test to runtime/tests/concurrent_eval.rs. Build a template with meta block and a Let using MetaAccess, run through edit_param_concurrent, assert the meta value resolves correctly.; F16 — Verify test passes (it should pass with current code since meta_map is already wired). If it fails, fix the wiring. This establishes a regression test before refactoring.; F15 — Write unit tests for ConcurrentEvalAdapter: (a) `from_setup_maps_all_fields` — verify all fields are correctly transferred from ConcurrentEditSetup; (b) `into_result_and_build_result_shared_equivalent` — verify both result-building paths produce identical output; (c) `evaluate_skips_constraint_nodes` — verify non-Value nodes return Unchanged; (d) `evaluate_missing_node_returns_unchanged` — verify graceful handling of nodes not in graph.; F15 — Verify all 4 new unit tests pass with current code. Fix any that don't (they should all pass since they test existing behavior).; F6 — Write test: field eval deduplication doesn't change behavior. Add `eval_field_analytical_and_composed_identical_path` test to meta_eval_integration.rs verifying both Analytical and Composed field sources evaluate correctly with meta_map.; F6 — Deduplicate Analytical/Composed field source branches in eval(). Merge into a single match arm: `Analytical { expr } | Composed { expr } => { ... }`. Verify test from step-5 passes.; F9 — No new test needed (refactor only). Verify existing tests still pass after adding make_eval_ctx helper.; F9 — Extract `make_eval_ctx` helper method on Engine: `fn make_eval_ctx<'a>(&'a self, values: &'a ValueMap, functions: &'a [CompiledFunction]) -> reify_expr::EvalContext<'a>` that returns `EvalContext::new(values, functions).with_meta(&self.meta_map)`. Replace all 31 inline EvalContext construction sites in lib.rs. Does NOT apply to evaluate_let_bindings (which takes meta_map as param) or compile_geometry_op (free function).; F7+F8 — No new tests needed (refactor only). Verify existing guard eval tests pass after extracting helper.; F7+F8 — Extract `evaluate_guarded_members` helper that takes a slice of member IDs (or ValueCellDecls), guard polarity (true/false), values, functions, meta_map, and snapshot, and evaluates or deactivates members accordingly. Use in both eval() (lines 1052-1100) and edit_param() guard re-elaboration (lines 1820-1860). Reduces ~80 lines of near-identical code to ~20.; F10+F11 — No new tests needed (optimization only). Verify existing guarded_groups and collection_subs tests pass after removing clones.; F10+F11 — Eliminate eager Vec clones in edit_param(). For guarded_groups (line 1813): collect guard_cell IDs into a small Vec<ValueCellId> first, then iterate groups by index. For collection_subs (line 1885): collect count_cell IDs and changed status into a small Vec, then iterate by index. Both avoid cloning the full struct vectors.; F4 — No new tests needed (refactor only). Verify existing let-binding tests pass after eliminating meta_map clones.; F4 — Eliminate 3 meta_map clones at evaluate_let_bindings call sites. Refactor evaluate_let_bindings to be a free function (not &mut self method) that takes all needed mutable state as explicit parameters: (&mut EventJournal, &mut CacheStore, template, values, snapshot, version_id, functions, meta_map). This avoids the &self.meta_map vs &mut self borrow conflict.; F12 — No new tests needed (minor optimization). Verify concurrent eval tests pass.; F12 — In edit_param_concurrent(), borrow eval_set and traces from setup by reference instead of cloning. Change scheduler.execute() call to use &setup.eval_set and &setup.traces. Requires checking if ConcurrentScheduler::execute takes owned or borrowed — if owned, pass setup.eval_set.clone() only where consumed.; F5 — No new tests needed. Verify edit_check_concurrent tests pass after reducing clones.; F5 — In edit_check_concurrent(), restructure to avoid triple-clone of resolved_params, diagnostics, values before apply. Extract needed data from result before consuming: move resolved_params and diagnostics out of result (using std::mem::take or destructuring) instead of cloning, then pass the stripped result to apply_concurrent_edit.; F1+F3 — Write benchmark-style test: `concurrent_eval_large_graph_no_quadratic_clone` — build a 100-node linear chain, run concurrent eval, assert completion (establishes baseline for ValueMap clone optimization).; F1 — Optimize ValueMap clone in ConcurrentEvalAdapter::evaluate(). Replace full ValueMap clone (line 201) with targeted reads: extract only the ValueCellIds referenced in the expression's dependency trace, building a small local ValueMap. Use extract_dependency_trace(expr) to get the reads list, then lock values briefly to read only those entries. This reduces O(n) clone to O(k) where k is the expression's arity (typically 1-3).; F13+F14 — Document async safety as design decisions (no code change). Verify all tests pass as final confirmation.; F13+F14 — Add doc comments to ConcurrentEvalAdapter::evaluate() explaining: (a) the stale-snapshot read is safe because the scheduler enforces topological ordering — all predecessors complete before a node is scheduled; (b) the three separate lock acquisitions are safe because no other task reads a node's value until that node's evaluate() returns, enforced by the scheduler's dependency tracking. No code change, documentation only.
Debug fix for: Failures: lint issues

Define MetaMap type alias `pub type MetaMap = HashMap<String, HashMap<String, String>>` in reify-eval/src/lib.rs (near imports) to reduce verbosity across both crates. Update ConcurrentEditSetup.meta_map field type and Engine.meta_map field type to use MetaMap.

Completed all remaining steps (5-8): step-5 added edit_check_concurrent_meta_access_resolves and into_result_fallback tests; step-6 changed ConcurrentEditSetup.meta_map to Arc<MetaMap> eliminating deep clone in from_setup; step-7 renamed misleading performance test; step-8 extracted eval_ctx free function and converted all 15 remaining EvalContext::new sites + added doc comments. All 464 tests pass across both crates, full workspace compiles cleanly.
